summary: Soundboard capture of a late‑starting but stellar 12/4/73 set, highlighted by an epic Eyes of the World and a killer Truckin’ > Stella Blue transition.
review: The Miller SBD recording is generally crisp and clear, with only a slight balance hiccup on “One More Saturday Night.” Musically the show shines – a dark, soaring Eyes of the World, a jaw‑dropping Truckin’→Stella Blue segue, and a joyous finish with Sugar Magnolia make it one of the fall ’73 tour’s standout performances.

ai_rating.rationale: All five reviews are 5‑star and overwhelmingly praise the performance; only a minor recording balance issue is noted, leading to an excellent overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Miller board capture is generally crisp and well‑balanced; a slight mix imbalance is mentioned on “One More Saturday Night,” but the recording remains very listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert opened late and ran short, but quickly settled into a happy early set before diving into a deep, dark middle (notably Eyes of the World). The band resurfaced with an uplifting Sugar Magnolia and closed on a high note.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s shredding on “Johnny B. Goode” and his soaring lead during Eyes of the World are repeatedly praised; he trades a sci‑fi duet with Phil.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lesh’s bass is highlighted for its “bombs” that lock in with Jerry’s lead during the extended Eyes of the World jam.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s rhythm work shines on “Sugar Magnolia,” where his picking lifts the song after the long jam.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith Godchaux adds tasteful chords and ornaments throughout, especially seasoning the Eyes of the World jam.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann is described as “jackhammering” the snare, driving the intense middle section of Eyes of the World.
